title: BACI evaluation Baviaanskloof
text: use part Baviaanskloof dataset used del Río-Mena et al., 2021, included spatialBACI package, demonstrate pixel-based --control-impact (BACI) evaluation using package. Baviaanskloof dataset contains sites large-scale spekboom (Portulacaria afra) revegetation semi-arid Baviaanskloof Hartland Bawarea Conservancy, South-Africa. Attributes vector dataset indicate year revegetation undertaken different restoration organizations active area (Commonland, Living Land, Grounded) whether lifestock grazing exclusion implemented restoration activity. example, select sites revegetated 2012 subject lifestock grazing exclusion.
code: data(baviaanskloof) baviaanskloof <- unwrap(baviaanskloof)  year <- 2012 impact_sites <- baviaanskloof[baviaanskloof$Planting_date==year & baviaanskloof$Lifestock_exclusion==0] other_sites <- baviaanskloof[baviaanskloof$Planting_date!=year | baviaanskloof$Lifestock_exclusion==1]  plot(baviaanskloof, "Planting_date", xlab="Longitude", ylab="Latitude") lines(impact_sites, lwd=2)

title: BACI evaluation Baviaanskloof
text: spatialBACI package requires user define spatial unit analysis performed. options pixel unit (using raster-based analysis) polygon/point unit (using vector-based analysis). latter can useful working extensive networks fixed monitoring sites control sites can expected. One advantages pixel unit required candidate control sites predefined. Another advantage , depending size impact sites spatial resolution used remotely sensed datasets, information impact within impact site can assessed. example use pixels spatial unit analysis reporting. using raster-based analysis, first choice user make spatial resolution pixel units. choose spatial resolution 60m, since resolution several Earth Observation datasets (e.g., Landsat, Sentinel-2) can aggregated. next step identify area control pixels --Control-Impact selected. spatialBACI package provides several options . first option providing spatial window around impact units, second providing polygon identifying area select control pixels. package also provides options exclude areas use control BACI analysis. exclusion can based user-provided polygons, buffer area around impact sites (e.g., eliminate effect misregistration impact sites positive negative adjacency effects). options include exclude candidate control units can combined based user’s terrain knowledge data availability create_control_candidates() function. example, set window 5 km around sites reforested 2012 (control_from_buffer argument) exclude areas restored years control pixels (control_exclude argument). Finally, use argument exclude_impact_buffer exclude pixels buffer 300 m around impact sites selection control units. create_control_candidates function also allows us specify Coordinate Reference System (trough crs argument) reference raster analysis done. can useful , e.g., user wants use national reference system. example don’t provide crs parameter, case UTM zone centroid impact sites used. Setting round_coords argument -2 sets origin output SpatRaster closest 100 m, setting sample_control argument 0.5 results random sampling 50% candidate control pixels, can useful reduce computational load large study areas.  resulting plot shows impact units/pixels (1), candidate control units/pixels (0), pixels excluded analysis white. Notice plot shows UTM coordinates, rather geographic coordinates Baviaanskloof reference dataset. general, spatialBACI package automatically check crs used spatial datasets reproject required.
code: impact_control_cands <- create_control_candidates(impact_sites,                                                   resolution=60,                                                   control_from_buffer=5000,                                                   control_from_include=NULL,                                                   control_exclude=other_sites,                                                   exclude_impact_buffer=300,                                                   round_coords=-2,                                                   sample_control=0.5)  plot(impact_control_cands) lines(project(impact_sites, impact_control_cands))

title: BACI evaluation Baviaanskloof
text: impact assessment using control-impact (--control-impact) schemes, crucial control units carefully selected characteristics differ impact group received “treatment” (case restoration intervention). spatialBACI package done specifying set covariates used matching analysis. Ideally, users include “covariates likely impact selection treatment outcome interest” (Schleicher et al., 2019). Baviaanskloof example, use limited number matching covariates. restricted search window control units relatively small area around impact sites, assume climatic variation small mostly induced local topography. Topographic slope aspect may also determine whether site selected revegetation determine success revegetation intervention. terrain data can extracted NASA Digital Elevation Model (DEM) based SRTM data using dem() function. Unless specified otherwise, DEM obtained Microsoft’s Planetary Computer. user can also specify terrain derivatives calculated (default slope aspect), whether aspect transformed “northness” “eastness” applying cosine sine function (default) .  also included land cover matching covariate. ensure control pixel selected landcover type (.e. “rangeland”). Note current implementation lulc() function, 9-class land use land cover layer Impact Observatory used. product first generated year 2017, assume change land cover change intervention year (2012) 2017. Given terrain revegetated spekboom still classified “rangeland” issue example, may problem situations.  Distance roads settlements can explain anthropogenic pressure area. spatialBACI package therefore includes functions calculate distance nearest road (osm_distance_roads()) nearest settlement (osm_distance_places()) based OpenStreetMap data. example, include distance nearest road OSM category “track” higher environmental covariate matching analysis.  different matching covariates can easily collated format can serve input control-impact matching collate_matching_layers() function. function handles necessary spatial processing different spatial input layers, may different coordinate systems spatial resolutions. function test_multicollinearity() plots correlation matrix matching covariates, reports Variance Inflation Factor (VIF), allows user iteratively identify remove highly multicollinear covariates. example, VIF values covariates low (<5), choose keep matching. perform control-impact matching matchCI() function using propensity score matching (default method), select 10 control pixels impact pixel (argument ratio) replacement (argument replace), meaning control pixel can paired several impact pixels. function evaluate_matching() performs standard assessments matching output. default parameters, function generates plots covariate distribution, Love plot Standardized Mean Differences control impact sites matching. output matching analysis, can see absolute Standardized Mean Difference matching threshold 0.1 propensity score distance, barely threshold individual covariates. Based outputs can continue analysis current matching output, , reject matching attempt matching different matching parameters covariates.
code: dem_matching <- dem(impact_control_cands) plot(dem_matching) landcover_matching <- lulc(impact_control_cands, year=year) #> Warning in .local(x, year, endpoint, collection, assets, authOpt, ...): Land #> cover product io-lulc-9-class currently has data 2017-2022. 2017 selected. plot(landcover_matching) roadsDist_matching <- osm_distance_roads(impact_control_cands, values="track+") plot(roadsDist_matching) matching_input = collate_matching_layers(impact_control_cands, vars_list=list(dem_matching, landcover_matching, roadsDist_matching)) matching_input = test_multicollinearity(matching_input) matching_output <- matchCI(matching_input,                             ratio=10, replace=TRUE) evaluate_matching(matching_output)  #> Balance Measures #>                           Type Diff.Adj #> distance              Distance  -0.0000 #> elevation              Contin.   0.0488 #> slope                  Contin.  -0.1147 #> northness              Contin.  -0.0086 #> eastness               Contin.   0.0721 #> landcover_Trees         Binary   0.0000 #> landcover_Crops         Binary   0.0000 #> landcover_Built area    Binary   0.0000 #> landcover_Bare ground   Binary   0.0000 #> landcover_Rangeland     Binary   0.0000 #> dist_roads             Contin.   0.0471 #>  #> Sample sizes #>                       Control Treated #> All                  20436.       691 #> Matched (ESS)         3569.68     691 #> Matched (Unweighted)  4614.       691 #> Unmatched            15822.         0 #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue.

title: BACI evaluation Baviaanskloof
text: chose Landsat Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) metric use impact evaluation. NDVI indicative vegetation “greenness” therefore inform us whether large scale revegetation effort successful. Landsat programme running several decades therefore useful data source impact evaluation past interventions. Landsat data available several cloud platforms. use Microsoft’s Planetary Computer, allows querying SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) language. timeframes conservation restoration impacts assessed depend application study site. Assuming want evaluate impact Baviaanskloof revegetation 5-year periods 5-year intervention year, can first generate yearly NDVI time series impact candidate control pixels: code, specified want make pixel-based NDVI composite image year, using pixel year median value images acquired months March May cloud cover 60%. function eo_VI_yearly.stac() generates proxy data cubes, don’t contain data describe dimension object. Data retrieval calculations performed data values contained objects needed. Working data cubes advantage unnecessary calculations avoided bands, time steps /coordinates used later steps. can extremely useful dealing large areas limited data extracted, e.g., sparse monitoring location distributed continental-scale area. case want process data wall--wall continental scale, sparse locations. Baviaanskloof example, however, cover geographically small area relatively dense cover impact candidate control units. case can useful transform data cubes SpatRaster objects (terra package) .SpatRaster() function. downloads processes data reads resulting composite images memory (writes temporal files).   transform time series simple metrics can used BACI analysis, calculate average value trend (change NDVI/year) time series intervention calc_ts_metric() function. basic time series analysis included spatialBACI package, user can easily implement analyses, long return raster layers evaluation variable interest intervention.
code: stac_endpoint <- as.endpoint("PlanetaryComputer") stac_collection <- as.collection("Landsat", stac_endpoint) vi_before <- eo_VI_yearly.stac(impact_control_cands, "NDVI",                                endpoint=stac_endpoint, collection=stac_collection,                                years=seq(year-10, year-1, 1),                                months = 3:5,                                maxCloud=60) vi_after <- eo_VI_yearly.stac(impact_control_cands, "NDVI",                               endpoint=stac_endpoint, collection=stac_collection,                               years=seq(year+1, year+10, 1),                               months = 3:5,                               maxCloud=60) vi_before <- as.SpatRaster(vi_before) vi_after <- as.SpatRaster(vi_after) plot(vi_before, main=paste0("NDVI - ",seq(year-10, year-1, 1))) plot(vi_after, main=paste0("NDVI - ",seq(year+1, year+10, 1))) avgtrend_before <- calc_ts_metrics(vi_before) avgtrend_after <- calc_ts_metrics(vi_after)

title: BACI evaluation Baviaanskloof
text: run BACI analysis, need provide matched control-impact pairs (output matchCI()) raster datasets period BACI_contrast() function. BACI analysis performed layers layers, example average trend time series. results BACI analysis can viewed printing data.table associated output object. data.table shows, impact unit evaluation variable, BACI contrast p-values. BACI contrast values smaller zero indicate positive effect revegetation intervention. BACI results can also plotted map, non-significant BACI contrast masked . desired, pixel-based results can now aggregated different spatial levels, e.g., revegetation site.
code: baci_results <- BACI_contrast(matching_output, before=avgtrend_before, after=avgtrend_after) head(baci_results$data) #> Key: <subclass> #>    subclass  cell treatment contrast.average p_value.average contrast.trend #>      <fctr> <int>     <num>            <num>           <num>          <num> #> 1:        1 18158         1     -0.007516017     0.107309678  -5.215998e-06 #> 2:        2 18373         1     -0.007124912     0.001719528  -1.234128e-06 #> 3:        3 18374         1     -0.006553027     0.036084266  -1.076089e-06 #> 4:        4 18375         1     -0.000707540     0.871349236  -3.731212e-06 #> 5:        5 18588         1     -0.002613761     0.677131688  -7.347847e-06 #> 6:        6 18589         1     -0.002799080     0.489423545  -6.619877e-06 #>    p_value.trend #>            <num> #> 1:    0.14678868 #> 2:    0.66389019 #> 3:    0.57750778 #> 4:    0.10941389 #> 5:    0.03418171 #> 6:    0.02101184 plot(baci_results$spat["contrast.average"] |> trim(),              range=baci_results$spat["contrast.average"] |> minmax() |> abs() |> max() |> rep(2)*c(-1,1),              col=hcl.colors(50, palette = "RdYlBu", rev = TRUE)) maskImg <- classify(baci_results$spat["p_value.average"], rcl=  matrix(ncol=3, data= c(0,0.05, NA, 0.05,1,1), byrow = TRUE)) plot(maskImg,              col=c('grey90'),             add=TRUE,              legend=FALSE) lines(project(impact_sites, baci_results$spat))

title: wildlife_circles_Finland
text: illustrate spatialBACI package can used pre-defined monitoring sites, use wildlife_circles dataset, simplified subset data used Terraube et al., 2020. dataset simplification Finnish wildlife triangles monitoring scheme (Helle et al., 2016), circle designed pass three vertices triangle made transects 4 km. wildlife_circles dataset provides outline circles polygons generic “ID” assigned, indicates circles whether located protected area (according World Database Protected Areas) (value 1/0 attribute “PA”).  Terraube et al., 2020, authors want match wildlife circles inside protected areas control sited outside protected areas using six matching covariates: Latitude Longitude Distance closest settlement Terrain ruggedness Human population density Percentage forest cover show perform analysis using spatialBACI package common R packages.
code: data(wildlife_circles) wildlife_circles <- unwrap(wildlife_circles)  wildlife_circles #>  class       : SpatVector  #>  geometry    : polygons  #>  dimensions  : 671, 2  (geometries, attributes) #>  extent      : 191977.9, 727420.1, 6665446, 7757954  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #>  coord. ref. : ETRS89 / TM35FIN(E,N) (EPSG:3067)  #>  names       :    ID    PA #>  type        : <int> <int> #>  values      :     1     0 #>                    2     0 #>                    3     0 plot(centroids(wildlife_circles), "PA", )

title: wildlife_circles_Finland
text: Extracting longitude latitude centroids polygons SpatVector object, case , rather straightforward using terra::.data.frame() functions terra package, spatialBACI package builds. following returns data frame wildlife circle original “ID” “PA” columns, additionally columns “x” “y” indicating longitude latitude centroid wildlife circle. Distance closest settlement can obtained using osm_distance_places function, define settlements OpenStreetMap features category “village” . set timeout argument 100 (seconds) allow retrieval settlements Finland. Terrain ruggedness Terraube et al. defined standard deviation elevation within wildlife triangles, included large carnivores expected reach higher densities rugged terrain. Finland mostly north area covered SRTM DEM, derive terrain ruggedness ALOS DEM freely available Planetary Computer STAC catalog. specify want use original 30m resolution x y ALOS DEM. specific function spatialBACI package extract human population density. However, data raster format stored locally accessible online can also easily integrated. example, access WorldPop population density Finland year 2016 (year used Terraube et al., 2020) 1km resolution, found https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=41175. Percentage forest cover calculated Terraube et al. (2020) CORINE Land Cover (CLC) dataset year 2012. downloaded CLC dataset year 2012 Finnish Environment Institute (SYKE) website (CORINE Land Cover 2012, 20 m geotiff (zip) (latest update 30.9.2014)) downloaded unzipped local directory D:/Data. Extracting forest fraction wildlife circle requires knowledge values forest classes stored (case values 22 29), custom function forestFrac_function. collate_matching_layers() function designed convert matching covariates different formats (data.frame, SpatVector, online locally stored rasters). , provide inputs collate_matching_layers() SpatVector file candidate control impact sites (wildlife_circles), list matching variables. matching variables raster format, additional information must provided raster data summarized vector units analysis. case, raster dataset must provided named list element “data”, additional named list elements specify summarizing function. population density, want calculate interpolated value four 1km pixels adjacent centroids wildlife circles, can used adding list element method="bilinear". Surface roughness can derived DEM setting list element fun=sd (na.rm=TRUE ignore occasional missing values DEM). Finally, formula derive forest fraction linked CLC raster dataset. step may take minutes, DEM data step downloaded processed. resulting object, column names data.table referring raster objects simply taken layer names corresponding file. clarity, can update column names (though contain spaces).
code: LonLat <- centroids(wildlife_circles) |> as.data.frame(geom="XY") dist_village <- osm_distance_places(x=wildlife_circles, values="village+", timeout=100, osm_bbox = "Finland") dem_finland <- dem(x=wildlife_circles,                  v="elevation",                  dem_source=list(endpoint="https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/api/stac/v1",                                  collection="alos-dem",                                  assets="data"),                  dx=30, dy=30) popdens_url <- "https://data.worldpop.org/GIS/Population_Density/Global_2000_2020_1km/2016/FIN/fin_pd_2016_1km.tif" clc_filename <- file.path("D:/Data/spatialBACI","clc2012_fi20m.tif") forestFrac_function <- function(x){sum(x %in% 22:29)/length(x)} vars_list <- list(LonLat,                    dist_village,                    list(data=clc_filename,                        fun=forestFrac_function),                   list(data=popdens_url,                        method="bilinear"),                   list(data=dem_finland,                        fun=sd,                         na.rm=TRUE)                   )  matching_input_vector <- collate_matching_layers(wildlife_circles, vars_list=vars_list) matching_input_vector #> $data #>         ID    PA      x       y dist_places  OBJECTID fin_pd_2016_1km elevation #>      <int> <int>  <num>   <num>       <num>     <num>           <num>     <num> #>   1:     1     0 525952 7074646    6700.571 0.7325014       0.9526159 10.595218 #>   2:     2     0 292691 6789509    2832.911 0.7143827       2.2052165  9.535269 #>   3:     3     0 224286 6810768    3649.480 0.7311088       3.0462480  8.666103 #>   4:     4     1 243257 6882305    4978.423 0.4057153       0.8412691  5.335253 #>   5:     5     0 259491 6851807    1804.612 0.7654075       2.7937382  5.258316 #>  ---                                                                            #> 667:   667     1 579276 7539446   50800.176 0.8995540       0.1023321  9.173672 #> 668:   668     0 550461 6838690    5149.095 0.7110290       2.1137298 13.109967 #> 669:   669     0 406736 7342837   12476.489 0.8474569       0.7097588  8.576482 #> 670:   670     0 539159 7623874    7862.777 0.9268883       0.3741452 33.181941 #> 671:   671     0 287663 6681534    1653.141 0.6267782      12.1432007 12.311376 #>  #> $spat.ref #>  class       : SpatVector  #>  geometry    : polygons  #>  dimensions  : 671, 2  (geometries, attributes) #>  extent      : 191977.9, 727420.1, 6665446, 7757954  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #>  coord. ref. : ETRS89 / TM35FIN(E,N) (EPSG:3067)  #>  names       :    ID    PA #>  type        : <int> <int> #>  values      :     1     0 #>                    2     0 #>                    3     0 names(matching_input_vector$data)[3:8] <- c("easting", "northing", "dist_villages", "forest_fraction" ,"pop_density", "terrain_roughness")

title: wildlife_circles_Finland
text: match one wildlife circle outside protected area site within protected area without replacement using collated matching covariates, simply provide resulting object matchCI() function. must provide column names defining unique ID spatial unit analysis (colname.id="ID") treatment (colname.treatment="PA"). example, use 1:1 matching without replacement. Matching results can now evaluated using evaluate_matching(), user can decide continue analysis matched dataset repeat matching different parameters.
code: matching_output_vector <- matchCI(matching_input_vector,                                   colname.id="ID", colname.treatment="PA",                                   ratio=1, replace=FALSE, method="nearest") evaluate_matching(matching_output_vector) #> Balance Measures #>                       Type Diff.Adj #> distance          Distance   0.0495 #> easting            Contin.   0.0541 #> northing           Contin.  -0.0825 #> dist_villages      Contin.  -0.0373 #> forest_fraction    Contin.   0.3075 #> pop_density        Contin.  -0.1238 #> terrain_roughness  Contin.   0.0075 #>  #> Sample sizes #>           Control Treated #> All           610      61 #> Matched        61      61 #> Unmatched     549       0 #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue. #> Press <Enter> to continue.

title: Spatial Before-After-Control-Impact assessment
text: spatialBACI package aims simplify counterfactual impact evaluation ecosystem conservation/restoration actions, using geospatial datasets raster vector format. main possibilities package include: Identification candidate control impact units analysis Control-impact matching matching evaluation Impact evaluation using --Control-Impact framework

title: Spatial Before-After-Control-Impact assessment
text: latest version spatialBACI package can installed GitHub using devtools package:

title: As bounding box — as.bbox
text: Create Bounding Box SpatRaster, SpatVector, SpatExtent object target CRS. Bounding box 4-element vector order c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)

title: As bounding box — as.bbox
code: as.bbox(x, ...)  # S4 method for class 'SpatRaster' as.bbox(x, crs_to = crs("epsg:4326"))  # S4 method for class 'SpatVector' as.bbox(x, crs_to = crs("epsg:4326"))  # S4 method for class 'SpatExtent' as.bbox(x, crs_from = crs("epsg:4326"), crs_to = crs("epsg:4326"))  # S4 method for class 'numeric' as.bbox(   x,   ...,   crs_from = crs("epsg:4326"),   crs_to = crs("epsg:4326"),   xy = TRUE )

title: As bounding box — as.bbox
text: x SpatRaster, SpatVector, SpatExtent, numeric ... x numeric, additional values ymin, xmax, ymax crs_to character. Description Coordinate Reference System created bounding box, PROJ.4, WKT authority:code notation. Defaults longitude/latitude. crs_from x CRS attached (.e., x SpatExtend numeric), character description Coordinate Reference System corresponding calues x. Defaults longitude/latitude. xy logical. x numeric vector length 4 (4 numeric values), set parameter TRUE coordinates order (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) FALSE order (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax).

title: As bounding box — as.bbox
text: numeric vector length four

Possible values:  * "lpdaac" = "https://cmr.earthdata.nasa.gov/stac/LPCLOUD",  * "lpcloud" = "https://cmr.earthdata.nasa.gov/stac/LPCLOUD",  * "planetarycomputer" = "https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/api/stac/v1",  * "planetary computer" = "https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/api/stac/v1"

title: Before-After-Control-Impact contrast — BACI_contrast
text: Calculate (--)Control-Impact contrast p-value [Meroni et al. (2017)](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jag.2017.02.016) [del Río-Mena et al. (2021)](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0243020)

title: Before-After-Control-Impact contrast — BACI_contrast
code: BACI_contrast(   ci_matches,   before,   after,   effect,   colname.id,   colname.treatment,   colname.subclass = "subclass",   fun,   method = "simple",   ... )

title: Before-After-Control-Impact contrast — BACI_contrast
text: ci_matches list. Control-impact pairs spatial reference, output [matchCI()] SpatRaster data.cube pre-intervention observable interest SpatRaster data.cube post-intervention observable interest effect SpatRaster data.cube effect observable interest. See Details. colname.id character. Name column containing unique ID spatial unit analysis `ci_matches`. Defaults "cell" SpatRaster input `ci_matches`. colname.treatment character. Name column containing treatment variable `ci_matches`. Defaults "treatment" SpatRaster input `ci_matches`. colname.subclass character. Name column containing subclass assignemt `ci_matches`. Defaults "subclass". fun function summarize extracted data line polygon geometries. See [terra::extract()]. method character. Method extracting values point geometries. See [terra::extract()]. ... additional arguments `fun`.

title: Before-After-Control-Impact contrast — BACI_contrast
text: list (See Details)

title: Before-After-Control-Impact contrast — BACI_contrast
text: "effect" raster variable interest can provided instead pre-intervention post-intervention variable, effect variable takes priority provided. function returns list named elements `data`, containing (BA)CI contrast p-value impact unit analysis data.table, `spat`, containing data spatial object.

title: Before-After-Control-Impact contrast — BACI_contrast
text: Meroni, M., Schucknecht, ., Fasbender, D., Rembold, F., Fava, F., Mauclaire, M., Goffner, D., Di Lucchio, L.M., Leonardi, U., 2017. Remote sensing monitoring land restoration interventions semi-arid environments –control-impact statistical design. International Journal Applied Earth Observation Geoinformation 59, 42–52. del Río-Mena, T., Willemen, L., Vrieling, ., Snoeys, ., Nelson, ., 2021. Long-term assessment ecosystem services ecological restoration sites using Landsat time series. PLOS ONE 16, e0243020.

title: Baviaanskloof revegetation data — baviaanskloof
text: Data large scale planting Baviaanskloof, South Africa.

title: Baviaanskloof revegetation data — baviaanskloof
text: SpatVector object 118 geometries 2 attributes Lifestock_exclusion Integer indicating lifestock exclusion applied (1) (0) Planting_date Integer indicating year revegetation intervention (2005–2015)

title: Baviaanskloof revegetation data — baviaanskloof
text: Dataset provided T. del Río-Mena anonymized, full dataset supporting del Río-Mena et al. (2021) provided https://doi.org/10.17026/dans-zrc-hmz4.

title: Calculate Vegatation Index — calc_VI.cube

text: cube data cube spectral bands VI vegetation index scale scale source surface reflectance data

title: Calculate Vegatation Index — calc_VI.cube
text: proxy data cube object

title: Calculate Vegatation Index — calc_VI.cube
text: Implemented vegetation indices: NDVI = (nir-red)/(nir+red) NDMI = (nir-swir1)/(nir+swir1) NBR = (nir-swir2)/(nir+swir2) NBR2 = (swir1-swir2)/(swir1+swir2) NDSI = (green-swir1)/(green+swir1) EVI = 2.5*(nir-red)/(nir+6*red-7.5*blue+1) SAVI = 1.5*(nir-red)/(nir+red+0.5) MSAVI = (2*nir+1-sqrt((2*nir+1)^2-8*(nir-red)))/2 Scale factor applied reflectance saved integer instead range [0-1]. checked different endpoints

Alternatively, list elements can refer raster datasets (SpatRaster data cube). case, list element must list minimum list element named `data` corresponding raster dataset. Additional list elements can provided define raster data summarized vector units analysis `x`. geometries `x` polygons, summarizing function can provided additional list element named `fun` (e.g., `fun="mean"`). additional list elements can provided arguments `fun`, example `na.rm=TRUE`. geometries `x` points, additional list element named `method` can defined specify nearest neighbour (`method="simple"`) bilinear interpolation (`method="bilinear"`) extraction raster dataset. absence additional list element specifying `fun` `method`, nearest neighbour extraction point geometries performed, polygon centroids geometries `x` polygons. Combinations SpatVector, data.frame/data.table /raster/summarizing function pairs allowed. output object names list elements named `data` `spat.ref`. list element `data` contains data.table rows corresponding spatial units analysis defined `x`. `x` SpatRaster, columns indicate unique ID corresponding cell number (column named "cell"), whether unit analysis candidate control (0) impact (1) unit (column name specified `colname.treatment`), values different matching covariates (column names corresponding layer names matching covariates raster dataset). `x` SpatVector, columns refer unique ID treatment (defined `x` attributes), different matching covariates.

title: Create candidate control raster — create_control_candidates
text: Create SpatRaster impact/treatment candidate control pixels.

title: Create candidate control raster — create_control_candidates
code: create_control_candidates(   impact,   resolution,   crs,   control_from_buffer = NULL,   control_from_include = NULL,   control_exclude = NULL,   exclude_impact_buffer = NULL,   sample_control = NULL,   sample_impact = NULL,   round_coords = FALSE )

title: Create candidate control raster — create_control_candidates
text: impact SpatVector. Vector representation impact sites resolution numeric. Spatial resolution output SpatRaster, units `crs` crs Coordinate Reference System PROJ.4, WKT authority:code notation. Defaults UTM zone center coordinates `impact` control_from_buffer numeric vector 1, 2, 4 elements. Indicates much (units `crs`) spatial extent around impact sites enlarged side. control_from_include SpatVector. Indicates area include candidate control selection control_exclude SpatVector. Indicates area exclude candidate control selection exclude_impact_buffer numeric vector length 1 2. Indicates buffer around `impact`, units `crs`, exclude impact /control. See Details. sample_control numeric. positive integer giving number control units choose, value 0 1 giving fraction control units choose. sample_impact numeric. `sample_control`, impact units. round_coords logical integer. coordinates output SpatRaster rounded. TRUE, coordinates rounded nearest integer; positive numeric rounds corresponding decimal, negative integer rounds corresponding power 10 (e.g., `round_coords=-2` rounds nearest 100).

title: Create candidate control raster — create_control_candidates
text: SpatRaster impact pixels value 1, candidate control pixels value 0, pixels excluded impact control value NA

title: Create candidate control raster — create_control_candidates
text: Impact pixels defined providing SpatVector object. Pixels include candidate control units can defined SpatVector file, using `control_from_include` argument, bounding box around impact SpatVector object, using `control_from_buffer` argument. least one arguments must provided, provided `control_from_buffer` argument ignored. Pixels can excluded candidate control units providing SpatVector `control_exclude` argument. Additionally, pixels borders impact polygon can excluded impact /control `exclude_impact_buffer` argument, account adjacency effects. negative value `exclude_impact_buffer` eliminate pixels inner buffer around polygon impact units, positive value eliminate pixels outer buffer control units. Providing vector length two can used exclude inner outer buffer. large areas, impact candidate control pixels can randomly sampled population impact/control pixels, using `sample_impact` `sample_control` arguments, respectively. can useful reduce computational cost subsequent matching analysis.
